message:
fix(layout,parser): fix BK compaction double-shift and improve kanban indent detection

Layout (fm-layout, bk_horizontal_compaction):
- Fix double-application of class shifts: previously shifts were applied
  to all nodes, then coordinates were re-propagated which applied shifts
  again to non-root nodes via x[root[v]]
- Now apply shifts only to block roots first, then propagate (shifted)
  root coordinates to all block members in a single pass
- Removes 5 lines of redundant shift logic

Parser (fm-parser, parse_kanban):
- Replace fixed indent-level threshold (<=1 = column, 2+ = card) with
  dynamic indent tracking via current_column_indent
- Column headers detected by indent being <= the current column's indent
  or when no column has been established yet
- Use leading_indent_width() helper for consistent indent measurement
  across parsers (was using ad-hoc spaces/tabs calculation)
- Reset current_column_indent on parse errors for proper recovery

Beads:
- Close bd-3uz.5 (pressure signal adapters) as completed
- Mark bd-3uz.6 (budget broker) as in_progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3uz.27","title":"Emergency release-gate override protocol with auditable policy enforcement","description":"Define and enforce emergency override protocol for release gates.

Scope:
- Require explicit approver identity, reason, scope, and expiration for overrides.
- Log override events with immutable audit metadata.
- Add post-override follow-up requirements (retro, fix bead creation, temporary exceptions expiry).

Verification:
- Unit tests for override policy validation rules.
- Integration tests proving unauthorized/invalid overrides are rejected.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:28.744380711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:02.356903423Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","governance","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.27","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:28.744380711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.27","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.25","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:29.443707218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":92,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.27","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3uz.3","title":"Add runtime capability metadata surfaces and docs auto-generation gate","description":"Expose runtime capability metadata and auto-generate support documentation from the same source of truth.

Scope:
- Add capability metadata endpoint/surface for CLI and WASM.
- Generate README support tables and docs snippets from `capability_matrix.json`.
- Add CI checks preventing manual docs drift from runtime capability metadata.
- Include per-feature confidence/support level and links to test evidence.

Why this matters:
Users should not need to guess which features are production-ready versus best-effort.

Acceptance criteria:
- CLI command and WASM API expose capability metadata.
- Docs generation is reproducible and CI-enforced.
- README support claims are 100% generated or validated against the matrix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.123793197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:02:46.443014903Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T22:02:46.442553869Z","close_reason":"Implemented CLI/WASM capability metadata surfaces plus README drift gate","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","cli","docs","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.123793197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:35.549941211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:35.637874449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":90,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3uz.4","title":"Implement staged LayoutAlgorithm dispatch framework with capability-aware selection ledger","description":"Implement a staged `LayoutAlgorithm` dispatch framework so algorithm selection is real, deterministic, and capability-aware even while some algorithms are still in-flight.

Scope:
- Implement deterministic dispatch and selection ledger for currently available algorithm families.
- Emit explicit `capability_unavailable` diagnostics when a requested algorithm family is not yet implemented.
- Add deterministic fallback mapping so user intent is preserved and never silently misrepresented.
- Keep selection criteria auditable with stable tie-breaks and trace fields.

Why this matters:
Dispatch logic should become correct and observable immediately; it should not wait on every downstream algorithm implementation bead.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dispatch framework is active for available families with deterministic selection logs.
- Unavailable families produce explicit diagnostics and deterministic fallback behavior.
- Unit/integration tests verify dispatch determinism, fallback correctness, and diagnostics stability.
- E2E runs include structured dispatch logs (requested vs selected algorithm, reason, fallback path, timings).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.212977679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T20:55:23.006000520Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T20:55:23.005535150Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","layout","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.212977679Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:35.727242354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":69,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3uz.5","title":"Implement native+WASM compute-pressure signal adapters with deterministic tier quantization","description":"Create cross-surface pressure signal adapters that estimate available compute budget without introducing nondeterministic behavior.

Scope:
- Native adapter: process-level timing/memory pressure heuristics.
- WASM adapter: frame budget / event-loop lag / worker saturation proxies.
- Normalize signals into deterministic, quantized pressure tiers consumed by the budget broker.
- Document signal limitations and fallback behavior when telemetry is unavailable.

Why this matters:
Graceful degradation requires pressure awareness, especially on overloaded machines.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pressure tier inputs available on CLI and WASM surfaces.
- Signal quantization is deterministic and replayable.
- Missing telemetry paths degrade safely with explicit diagnostics.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.304183819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T05:50:22.962932125Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ops","runtime","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2u0.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:58:44.072941964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.304183819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":60,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency note: pressure-signal adapters require baseline WASM API surface (`bd-2u0.3`) rather than completion of the entire web epic; this keeps pressure-resilience work from being artificially blocked.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:58:44Z"},{"id":89,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:01Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3uz.6","title":"Implement cross-stage global budget broker for parse/layout/render","description":"Implement a global budget broker across parse/layout/render stages to enforce bounded runtime under pressure.

Scope:
- Allocate and enforce time/iteration/work budgets per stage.
- Expose deterministic arbitration policy when competing stages exceed budget.
- Integrate with layout time-budget guardrails and parser recovery budgets.
- Emit budget ledger events for observability and replay.

Why this matters:
Without a broker, stage-level safeguards are fragmented and overloaded runs can still destabilize.

Acceptance criteria:
- Hard budget enforcement exists across all core stages.
- Budget decisions are deterministic and logged.
- Synthetic worst-case corpus demonstrates bounded execution.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.390690384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59.337978232Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["guardrails","performance","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.390690384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.218900928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.305217236Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vb9.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.393120017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":68,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging.  {"id":"bd-3uz.7","title":"Create deterministic degradation-operator library for quality-vs-cost tradeoffs","description":"Build a deterministic degradation-operator library to reduce rendering/load cost while preserving structural correctness.

Scope:
- Operators: label elision, edge simplification, cluster collapse, routing simplification, animation/effect suppression.
- Define operator ordering and tie-break rules to preserve determinism.
- Ensure output remains semantically faithful and visibly marked as degraded where relevant.
- Add calibration knobs with safe defaults and reproducible behavior.

Why this matters:
Graceful degradation should be systematic, not ad hoc.

Acceptance criteria:
- Operator library integrated and test-covered.
- Degradation sequence is deterministic and replayable.
- Structural correctness invariants hold under all degradation tiers.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.480292207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59.194690822Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["degradation","layout","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.480292207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.572620811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.484452553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3uz.8","title":"Activate MermaidGuardReport and MermaidDegradationPlan across pipeline and surfaces","description":"Activate `MermaidGuardReport` and `MermaidDegradationPlan` end-to-end so configuration contracts in fm-core become runtime reality.

Scope:
- Populate guard/degradation structures during parse/layout/render.
- Ensure CLI/WASM surfaces receive and expose these structures.
- Add deterministic tests validating transitions between normal and degraded tiers.
- Add clear user-facing diagnostics explaining which safeguards fired and why.

Why this matters:
The core data contracts exist; they must be wired into the pipeline to deliver promised resilience.

Acceptance criteria:
- GuardReport and DegradationPlan are non-stub and populated in real runs.
- Transition paths are fully covered by unit/integration tests.
- Diagnostics include trigger conditions, chosen operator set, and resulting quality tier.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.569575745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59.054155665Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","fm-core","graceful-degradation","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2b4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.750618319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.569575745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:26.812781326Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:26.896447947Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:26.985011906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.662533146Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":55,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self rationale:  and  already exist in fm-core contracts; this task converts those definitions into live runtime behavior. Do not treat this as optional polish. It is the main reliability bridge for overloaded-machine scenarios.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:39Z"},{"id":58,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction note: `MermaidGuardReport` and `MermaidDegradationPlan` are explicit fm-core contracts that must be populated by live parser/layout/render execution. This task is the bridge from schema-only definitions to runtime-enforced graceful degradation and should be treated as core correctness work, not optional polish.","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:58:06Z"},{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3uz.9","title":"Add structured pressure/degradation diagnostics schema across CLI and WASM","description":"Define and emit structured pressure/degradation diagnostics so automated systems can reason about runtime behavior under stress.

Scope:
- Extend diagnostics schema with budget usage, pressure tier, degradation operators, and confidence.
- Emit in `fm-cli validate --format json` and trace bundle outputs.
- Expose equivalent data in WASM APIs for browser telemetry and debuggability.
- Add schema versioning and backward-compatible evolution rules.

Why this matters:
Graceful degradation is only operationally useful if it is machine-observable.

Acceptance criteria:
- Schema is documented and versioned.
- CLI and WASM emit consistent pressure diagnostics.
- Trace replay reproduces observed degradation decisions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.661368152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:01.844568701Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","schema","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2b4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.931674026Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:34.661368152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:57:36.839676013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":88,"issue_id":"bd-3uz.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:01Z"}]}
